title: Why make the Dead Sea Scrolls in the first place
display_title: Why make the Dead Sea Scrolls in the first place
wikitext:
[]: Why make the Dead Sea Scrolls in the first place? Post by blast335 » Mon Sep 07, 2015 9:36 am Ok so the whitemoon was sent to earth intentionally by an ancient alien race right? And the blackmoon was made by the same alien race but wasn't supposed to go to earth. If that's the case then why did that alien race make the dead sea scrolls? Why did they make a system that would cause instrumentality if adam and lillith came into contact?

[]: Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Sep 07, 2015 1:15 pm As a failsafe, in case that exact scenario (both arriving at the same place) happened. Instrumentality (as I understand it) was meant as a 'reset' button in case both progeny ended up competing for the same world (as are the Spears of Longinus, used to seal either of the Seeds to prevent them from 'activating' for the same purpose).

[]: Post by blast335 » Mon Sep 07, 2015 1:46 pm Ok but how were they able to translate them? They're; A. Older than life on earth, and B. Written in a language from a long dead alien race. There wasn't any language that descended from it, and since there was DEFINITELY not any communication between humanity and this race, there can't be a rosetta stone like device.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 07, 2015 1:51 pm There's some discussion about the "translation, how???" matter here that you'd probably find useful. It shows up about halfway through the thread.

[]: Post by Ray » Mon Sep 07, 2015 1:51 pm I assume it's like Lovecraft's Necronomicon. Where merely being in close proximity to them would warp and conform the mind of the reader so that he could read them. Y'know, I always wondered how that worked in the Evangelion universe. Were the prophets of the Bible just human beings with control over their AT fields?

[]: Post by blast335 » Mon Sep 07, 2015 2:18 pm Reichu wrote: There's some discussion about the "translation, how???" matter here that you'd probably find useful. It shows up about halfway through the thread.

[]: Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Sep 07, 2015 2:24 pm "Experiment #708: Translation Attempts for Dead Sea Scrolls. Method attempt: Licking the Scrolls. We drew straws, and Simonds is up for this one. Go get 'em Simonds." "I hate all of you."

[]: Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon Sep 07, 2015 2:28 pm ^ SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by blast335 » Mon Sep 07, 2015 3:30 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: "Experiment #708: Translation Attempts for Dead Sea Scrolls. Method attempt: Licking the Scrolls. We drew straws, and Simonds is up for this one. Go get 'em Simonds." "I hate all of you."

[]: Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Sep 07, 2015 3:39 pm Nope: the original word is German, which doesn't use diacriticals (they use umlauts instead). Further, resume lacks the diacritical in English (Germanic) but regains it in French (Latin).

[]: Post by blast335 » Mon Sep 07, 2015 4:04 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Nope: the original word is German, which doesn't use diacriticals (they use umlauts instead). Further, resume lacks the diacritical in English (Germanic) but regains it in French (Latin).

[]: Post by pwhodges » Mon Sep 07, 2015 5:19 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Nope: the original word is German, which doesn't use diacriticals (they use umlauts instead).

[]: Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Sep 07, 2015 10:56 pm ^ That would be that uppity Norman talk, there.

[]: Post by Bryan » Tue Sep 08, 2015 7:33 am Gob Hobblin wrote: "Experiment #708: Translation Attempts for Dead Sea Scrolls. Method attempt: Licking the Scrolls. We drew straws, and Simonds is up for this one. Go get 'em Simonds." "I hate all of you."

[]: Post by Gob Hobblin » Tue Sep 08, 2015 11:05 pm "Sometimes science is a lot more art, than science. A lot of people don't get that." -Rick Sanchez

[]: Post by Sachi » Wed Sep 09, 2015 2:18 am I suppose we're done here. Here's other threads for further reading: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/4071/Secret-Dead-Sea-ScrollsFAR/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13318/Why-is-there-only-one-set-of-Dead-Sea-Scrolls/
